title: Construction, Invariants, Dataclasses, and Slots · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Build trustworthy catalog, member, and loan objects from validated external records. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for validated library entity types. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish object allocation in __new__ from initialization in __init__ and override either only for justified behavior.
Establish complete valid object invariants during construction or reject invalid input with meaningful errors.
Implement classmethod alternate constructors that normalize external representations through one invariant path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python construction invariants. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python construction invariants. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

content.text: Design immutable value objects with coherent equality, hashing, normalization, and validation semantics. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Any object with an equals method is automatically safe as a dictionary key. This fails the Oop Value Objects contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Python Data Model Reference (Python 3.14.6) — Controls object identity, classes, descriptors, method binding, special methods, MRO, and runtime behavior.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Value Objects applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design immutable value objects with coherent equality, hashing, normalization, and validation semantics.
